cover.status_line: Confidential · For Internal Algolia Use Only

company_snapshot.revenue_source_label: The RealReal — Q3 2025 Earnings Press Release
company_snapshot.current_search_vendor: No detectable search vendor (Site Search widget REMOVED per BuiltWith)

executives.name: Rati Sahi Levesque
executives.title: President & CEO
executives.quote: We’re deeply rooted in data and behaviour analytics, personalizing our emails, our site experience.
executives.quote_source_label: Business of Fashion — State of Fashion 2026 Interview
executives.relevance: PRIMARY TARGET. She is the CEO and first employee — she knows the product deeply and has stated AI and personalization as strategic priorities. The gap between her stated ambition (‘personalizing our site experience’) and the observed reality (zero personalization in search) is the core pitch angle. She owns the P&L and the vendor decision.

executives.name: Ajay Gopal
executives.title: CFO
executives.quote: [Quote pending — research required for Ajay Gopal]
executives.relevance: ECONOMIC BUYER SUPPORT. CFO since Mar 2024. Frame ROI at $584 AOV — even 1% search conversion improvement = ~$1M+/year. With EBITDA margin at ~5.4%, he will respond to fast payback framing.

executives.name: Todd Suko
executives.title: CLO & Secretary; Interim CPO
executives.tenure: CLO since May 2020; Interim CPO since Aug 2025
executives.quote: [Quote pending — research required for Todd Suko]
executives.relevance: TECHNICAL GATEKEEPER. No CTO listed publicly. Todd holds the Interim CPO role — product and tech decisions may route through him before reaching Rati. Engage carefully: he is primarily a lawyer (CLO) holding an interim product seat, so keep the conversation outcome-focused, not technical.

intelligence_signals.text: CEO Rati Levesque: ‘AI is at the heart of our authentication process... Athena [covers] all product categories — every watch, every handbag, every item.’ But searching ‘authentication process’ returns 390 ‘Live The Process’ clothing items.
intelligence_signals.source_name: Business of Fashion — State of Fashion 2026 Interview
intelligence_signals.relevance: TRR’s #1 trust differentiator — Athena AI authentication — is completely undiscoverable via search. The CEO has invested in AI authentication but the discovery layer has no AI at all.

intelligence_signals.text: CEO: ‘We’re deeply rooted in data and behaviour analytics, personalizing our emails, our site experience.’ Browser audit found zero personalization signals in search or homepage for anonymous visitors.
intelligence_signals.source_name: Business of Fashion — State of Fashion 2026 Interview
intelligence_signals.relevance: Direct gap between CEO’s stated personalization priority and the observable product reality. Algolia Personalization closes this gap with behavioral re-ranking — no data science team required.

intelligence_signals.text: CEO: ‘My Closet — launching early 2026 — where you can manage your wardrobe and see resale values of items you own.’ Product Recommendations engine was REMOVED per BuiltWith.
intelligence_signals.source_name: Business of Fashion — State of Fashion 2026 Interview
intelligence_signals.relevance: My Closet needs a discovery engine to connect wardrobe items to marketplace listings. With recs REMOVED, TRR has no infrastructure to power ‘items like yours on sale’ or cross-category discovery. Algolia Recommend fills this vacuum directly.

intelligence_signals.text: CEO: ‘Cross-border is a big opportunity for us.’ Current traffic: 91.9% US. Engineering Manager for International Expansion role open (R11469).
intelligence_signals.source_name: Business of Fashion + TRR Careers Page
intelligence_signals.relevance: International expansion requires multi-language search, geo-specific merchandising, and global sub-50ms latency. Algolia’s 100+ data center infrastructure and multi-language NeuralSearch are directly aligned to this stated priority.

intelligence_signals.text: BuiltWith confirms: Site Search widget REMOVED (0 live, 1 dead). Product Recommendations REMOVED (0 live, 1 dead). AI analytics REMOVED (0 live, 2 dead). Three tool categories removed — a vacuum Algolia fills.
intelligence_signals.relevance: Three simultaneous removals create a total discovery-tech vacuum. No incumbent vendor to displace — this is a greenfield deployment opportunity. TRR is not satisfied with their previous vendor; they removed it without a replacement.

intelligence_signals.text: Zero open ‘search’ roles on TRR careers page. ML investment is entirely focused on pricing (Principal Applied Scientist, $249K–$277K) and personalization data pipelines — NOT search infrastructure.
intelligence_signals.relevance: Build-vs-buy risk for search is LOW. Zero search-specific hiring means TRR is not building it in-house. The Staff Data Engineer role explicitly mentions ‘realtime personalization’ — Algolia Events API feeds directly into this existing data pipeline investment.

intelligence_signals.text: Staff Data Engineer (R11914) job description mentions building ‘ML-powered personalization infrastructure’ and explicitly names Claude and Cursor as AI-coding tools — an AI-forward engineering culture.
intelligence_signals.source_name: TRR Careers — Staff Data Engineer R11914
intelligence_signals.relevance: TRR engineers are building the personalization data foundation. Algolia’s Events API and Personalization layer plugs into this pipeline — accelerating their roadmap without competing with their engineering investment.

intelligence_signals.text: 3 consecutive quarters of positive Adjusted EBITDA. FY2025 guidance raised to $37.7–$38.7M. Revenue +17% YoY (Q3 2025). First sustained profitability after years of losses.
intelligence_signals.source_name: The RealReal — Q3 2025 Earnings Press Release
intelligence_signals.relevance: Red Zone: ~5.4% EBITDA margin. Frame as ‘protect the profitability you just achieved’ not ‘big new investment.’ At $584 AOV, search improvements compound directly into EBITDA.

intelligence_signals.text: 55% of secondhand luxury purchases now through multiband platforms (TRR Resale Report 2025). Tariffs and economic uncertainty driving more shoppers to luxury resale — a structural tailwind for TRR.
intelligence_signals.relevance: TRR is growing because of macro conditions, but to capture this demand they need search that converts discovery into sales. Better search + tailwind = compounding revenue growth.

intelligence_signals.text: BuiltWith analysis: NONE of TRR’s five key competitors (Poshmark, Vestiaire Collective, Fashionphile, Rebag, What Goes Around NYC) currently use Algolia. First-mover advantage available.
intelligence_signals.relevance: Zero competitors use Algolia in the luxury resale vertical. TRR can deploy Algolia as a first-mover and differentiate on discovery quality before Vestiaire Collective (also search-vendor-less) acts first.

competitors.name: Poshmark
competitors.search_vendor: 1 live Site Search widget + AI widget
competitors.notes: Highest audience overlap with TRR (20% shared). Peer-to-peer model vs TRR’s managed consignment. Lower AOV but massive scale. Bounce rate 9.4pp worse than TRR.

competitors.name: Vestiaire Collective
competitors.search_vendor: Site Search REMOVED (0 live, 1 dead) — same displacement signal as TRR
competitors.notes: Closest TRR analog: luxury resale, European-origin, also missing a search vendor. Algolia first-mover in this vertical creates competitive moat before Vestiaire acts.

competitors.name: Fashionphile
competitors.search_vendor: 2 live Site Search widgets + AI analytics
competitors.notes: Luxury handbag specialist (not full fashion). Has active search tools and Shopify platform. Bounce rate 15.4pp worse than TRR despite having a search vendor — quality of their search may be inferior.

competitors.name: Rebag
competitors.notes: Handbag-focused luxury resale on Shopify. Has active search and personalization. Smaller scale but direct luxury resale competitor with better search infrastructure than TRR.

competitors.name: 1stDibs
competitors.notes: High-end luxury auction platform. 0.67 audience affinity with TRR. Different business model (auctions vs consignment) but overlapping luxury buyer base.

findings.title: Zero Federated Search — TRR’s #1 Differentiator Is Invisible
findings.expected_behavior: Content page about Athena AI authentication surfaced alongside (or instead of) product results
findings.actual_behavior: 390 ‘Live The Process’ clothing items returned. Title shown: ‘Authentication Process On Sale - Up To 90% Off’. Zero content about Athena AI authentication.
findings.impact_stat: 68% of consumers would return to a site with a good search experience
findings.prospect_description: TRR’s entire brand trust story — Athena AI authentication covering every watch, handbag, and item — cannot be discovered via search. When a buyer searches ‘return policy’ they get 1,198 ‘Return to Tiffany’ jewelry items. The product-only search engine treats TRR’s biggest trust signals as invisible.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Federated Search creates a unified index across products, help articles, brand content, and authentication guides. ‘Authentication process’ would surface both Athena content AND relevant authenticated items — turning TRR’s differentiator into a discovery moment.
findings.algolia_case_study_result: +37% search revenue increase with Algolia federated search and Rules

findings.title: NLP Completely Absent — Price Constraints Ignored
findings.expected_behavior: Red dresses priced under $500 returned; price constraint applied from natural language
findings.actual_behavior: 304,367 items returned. Price constraint completely ignored. Returns all items mentioning ‘red’, ‘dress’, or ‘500’ in any context.
findings.impact_stat: 72% of e-commerce sites have mediocre or broken search relevance
findings.prospect_description: Luxury buyers at $584 AOV express complex, multi-attribute queries. Every TRR item is unique — when NLP fails, that one-of-a-kind Chanel dress is never found and the sale is permanently lost. The inventory never returns to stock. With 500K+ unique items, NLP is not a nice-to-have: it’s the only way to match intent to unique inventory at scale.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia NeuralSearch parses ‘red dress under 500’ into structured filters: color=red, category=dresses, price<=500. Returns 200–1,000 relevant items, not 304,367 wrong ones. Price, color, material, and occasion constraints are understood from natural language.
findings.algolia_case_study_result: +37% search revenue; Algolia NeuralSearch resolves multi-attribute fashion queries

findings.title: Recommendation Engine Removed — Cross-Sell Vacuum
findings.tested_query: Chanel Classic Jumbo Single Flap Bag (PDP at $4,500)
findings.expected_behavior: ‘Frequently Bought Together’ with matching wallet/belt; ‘Customers Also Viewed’ with cross-category luxury items
findings.actual_behavior: Only 4 ‘Similar Items’ shown — ALL same brand, same type (Chanel Classic Jumbo Double Flap, $7,500–$8,500). No FBT, no cross-category discovery, no ‘Customers Also Viewed’.
findings.prospect_description: BuiltWith confirms the Product Recommendations tool was REMOVED (0 live, 1 dead). For unique luxury items where exact matches don’t exist, cross-category discovery is how $584 AOV grows to $800+. A buyer viewing a $4,500 Chanel bag needs to see matching wallets, shoes, and jewelry — not just slightly different versions of the same bag.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Recommend powers FBT (‘complete the look’ across categories), Related Items (‘similar bags in your size’), and Trending Items (‘most favorited right now’). For unique inventory, Algolia’s similarity model matches on attributes (brand, style, era, material) rather than exact SKU.
findings.algolia_case_study_result: +150% mobile order rate with Algolia Recommend and AI personalization

findings.title: Zero Observable Personalization — CEO Says Otherwise
findings.tested_query: Running search session as anonymous visitor
findings.expected_behavior: Behavioral re-ranking based on recent views; personalized homepage carousels (‘Recommended for You’)
findings.actual_behavior: Zero personalization signals detected on homepage or in search results. Generic content (‘Brands You’ll Love’ with standard luxury brands). No ‘Recommended for You’, no behavioral re-ranking.
findings.impact_stat: 80% of consumers prefer personalized shopping experiences; 10–15% revenue lift from personalization
findings.prospect_description: CEO stated in Feb 2026: ‘We’re deeply rooted in data and behaviour analytics, personalizing our emails, our site experience.’ The browser audit found zero personalization in on-site search. With 40M+ members and unique inventory, personalization is the mechanism that surfaces the right $584 item to the right buyer before they leave the site.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Personalization re-ranks search results per user based on behavioral signals (views, clicks, purchases). After a buyer browses Gucci bags, Gucci surfaces first in subsequent searches. The Staff Data Engineer’s ‘realtime personalization’ pipeline feeds directly into Algolia’s Events API.
findings.algolia_case_study_result: +150% mobile order rate; Gymshark personalization drives significant basket size increase

findings.title: Text-Only SAYT — No Visual Search Preview
findings.expected_behavior: Visual typeahead with product thumbnails, prices, and category shortcuts
findings.actual_behavior: Text-only autocomplete: ‘chanel’, ‘chanel bag’, ‘chanel earrings’, ‘chanel flap bag’. No images, no prices, no category navigation links.
findings.impact_stat: Search autocomplete is available at 80% of e-commerce sites, yet only 19% achieve highest performance
findings.prospect_description: For luxury buyers making $584 average purchase decisions, a visual preview of the Chanel bag in the typeahead is the moment that converts browsing to buying intent. Competing luxury platforms (Farfetch, Net-a-Porter) show rich visual SAYT. Text-only autocomplete is a 2015-era UX on a 2026 luxury marketplace.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia InstantSearch.js delivers federated visual SAYT: product thumbnails with price and condition, category shortcuts (‘Chanel Bags →’), and designer landing page links — all updating in <200ms.

findings.title: Empty State Shows No Popular or Trending Searches
findings.expected_behavior: Popular searches, trending items, curated suggestions alongside Recent Searches
findings.actual_behavior: Shows only ‘Recent Searches’ (previously searched terms) + ‘Recently Viewed’ (1 item). No Popular Searches, no Trending Now, no curated suggestions.
findings.impact_stat: 75% of users leave after experiencing no search results; 12% of searches return zero results
findings.prospect_description: With 40M+ members who return directly (53.3% direct traffic), the empty search state is the highest-engagement moment on the site. Showing only personal recents misses the opportunity to guide buyers toward trending luxury items, seasonal categories, and high-inventory discoveries.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Query Suggestions populates the empty state with top 10 trending searches (updated daily from analytics), seasonal recommendations, and curated designer spotlights. ‘Trending: Birkin dupes under 2000, Vintage Chanel’ turns the empty state into a discovery engine.

findings.title: No Brand Landing Experience from Search
findings.expected_behavior: Redirect to brand landing page with hero banner, brand story, curated Chanel collections
findings.actual_behavior: 66,276 product results with lowercase ‘chanel’ as heading. No brand hero, no auto-filter by designer applied, no brand story, no redirect to brand landing page.
findings.impact_stat: 41% of sites fail to fully support the search query types performed by users
findings.prospect_description: TRR’s value is in the authentication and curation of individual brands. A luxury buyer searching ‘Chanel’ wants a branded moment — not 66K unfiltered results. For a designer-focused marketplace, brand identity in search is a missed conversion opportunity on every brand-name query.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Rules Engine injects brand hero banners at the top of results for designer queries, auto-applies the Designer filter, and can redirect to curated brand landing pages. ‘Chanel’ becomes a branded shopping moment, not a raw product dump.

findings.title: No Analytics Signals — Trending, Bestseller, Most Searched
findings.expected_behavior: Trending, Bestseller, or Most Searched badges on product cards within results
findings.actual_behavior: Heart/favorite counts visible (passive social proof: 137, 102, 72 hearts), ‘Most Popular’ sort option available — but NO trending badges, NO bestseller labels, NO ‘most searched’ indicators.
findings.impact_stat: 93% of consumers have made a purchase after reading reviews or social signals
findings.prospect_description: The data exists — heart counts are being shown, ‘Most Popular’ sort implies popularity signals. But none of this is surfaced as active badges in search results. For unique luxury items, ‘Trending’ or ‘Most Favorited’ badges create urgency and social proof at the moment of discovery.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Analytics powers real-time ‘Trending’ badges (rising search and click volume in past 7 days), ‘Most Favorited’ (from heart count signals), and A/B testing of badge placements. No manual merchandising required.

findings.title: Excellent Typo Tolerance — 3/3 Luxury Brand Misspellings Handled
findings.actual_behavior: Chanel products correctly returned for ‘channel bag’, ‘louie vuitton’ (Louis Vuitton), and ‘gucchi’ (Gucci). 3/3 luxury brand typos silently corrected.
findings.prospect_description: This is a genuine strength. Luxury brand names are frequently misspelled, and TRR’s current search handles them correctly. This is the foundation that Algolia would preserve while adding NLP, personalization, and federated capabilities on top.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia’s fuzzy matching maintains this typo tolerance at sub-5ms API latency while adding NLP, personalization, and recommendations on top of the same solid foundation.

findings.title: Dynamic Facets Strength — Category-Aware Filters
findings.expected_behavior: Category-specific filter options surfaced per query type
findings.actual_behavior: Dynamic facets working: ‘watch’ → Watch Styles; ‘shoes’ → Shoe Sizes; ‘jewelry’ → Ring Sizes; ‘handbag’ → Condition. 4th filter pill changes per category context.
findings.prospect_description: This is a genuine strength. Dynamic facets indicate a thoughtful search architecture. Algolia would preserve and extend this with additional dynamic facet layers (material, era, authentication grade) and personalized facet ordering based on individual buyer preferences.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Dynamic Facets extends this strength with AI-powered facet ordering (surfaces the filters each buyer uses most frequently first) and personalized facet values — a buyer who always filters by ‘Excellent’ condition sees that option surfaced automatically.

gap_pairs.said_quote: AI is at the heart of our authentication process... Athena [covers] all product categories — every watch, every handbag, every item.

gap_pairs.algolia_angle: Algolia NeuralSearch understands semantic intent — searches like "red dress under 500" return relevant results, not 300K generic items.

engagement_phases.label: CEO-First (Now)
engagement_phases.message: Your CEO interview in BoF stated AI personalization and cross-border expansion as top 2026 priorities. Our audit found zero search personalization and no search vendor at all. We’d love to show you how Algolia extends your Athena AI story into product discovery.
engagement_phases.timing: Engage within 30 days — she’s 16 months into role and actively defining vendor strategy

engagement_phases.label: ROI Validation (30-Day Pilot)
engagement_phases.message: At $584 AOV and ~$98M search-addressable revenue, a 5% improvement = ~$4.9M/year. Propose a 30-day A/B test with clear conversion and AOV KPIs. Frame as protecting the 3 consecutive quarters of profitability.
engagement_phases.timing: After CEO buy-in — CFO approves budget for pilot scope

engagement_phases.label: Technical Integration (International Expansion Angle)
engagement_phases.message: As you build international search infrastructure, Algolia powers 100+ countries with multi-language NeuralSearch out of the box. Show how the Staff Data Engineer’s personalization pipeline feeds directly into Algolia Events API.
engagement_phases.timing: After commercial interest confirmed at CEO/CFO level

objection_counters.objection: We’re still profitable and don’t want a big vendor commitment
objection_counters.counter: A 30-day A/B pilot costs <$100K and proves the revenue impact before any long-term commitment. At $584 AOV, even 1,000 additional conversions = $584K. The pilot pays for itself.

objection_counters.objection: We’re building personalization in-house with our data team
objection_counters.counter: Your Staff Data Engineer is building the data pipeline — not the search relevance layer. Algolia’s Events API feeds directly into your existing pipeline. You’re not replacing their work; you’re giving it a search interface.

objection_counters.objection: Our search is custom-built and works well enough
objection_counters.counter: ‘Red dress under 500’ returns 304,367 items. ‘Authentication process’ returns Live The Process clothing. These are the queries your 1.024M active buyers are typing today.

objection_counters.objection: CEO has only been in role 16 months — not ready for vendor decisions
objection_counters.counter: She’s already 16 months in and has publicly stated her AI and personalization priorities. She removed the old search vendor — that’s an active decision, not inaction. The timing is right: she knows what the problems are and hasn’t committed to a solution yet.

icp_mapping.profile: Public luxury resale marketplace, thin margins (Red Zone), AI-first CEO, search vendor vacuum, My Closet launch imminent, cross-border expansion active

priority_to_product.discovery_question: Your CEO told BoF that AI authentication is at the heart of your business — but when we searched ‘authentication process’ on your site, we got 390 Live The Process clothing items. How can buyers discover the Athena authentication story?

priority_to_product.discovery_question: Your CEO said you’re deeply rooted in behaviour analytics and personalization — when we tested search as a new visitor, we found zero personalization signals. How is the team bridging the gap between email personalization and on-site search today?

priority_to_product.their_priority: ‘Cross-border is a big opportunity for us’ — CEO, international expansion

priority_to_product.their_priority: EBITDA positive for 3 consecutive quarters, guidance raised, $584 AOV (+12%)
priority_to_product.algolia_solution: ROI-focused 30-day A/B pilot on search + recommendations — fast measurables at thin margin
priority_to_product.discovery_question: You’ve achieved 3 consecutive quarters of profitability at $584 AOV. Our audit found 4 critical search gaps. At ~$98M search-addressable revenue, even a 5% improvement = ~$4.9M/year. A 30-day pilot would prove the impact without a long-term commitment.

anchor_lines: TRR’s CEO told BoF ‘AI is at the heart’ of their business — but searching ‘authentication process’ returns Live The Process clothing. Algolia Federated Search makes Athena’s story discoverable.
TRR says they’re ‘deeply rooted in behaviour analytics and personalization’ — our audit found ZERO personalization signals in search. Algolia Personalization closes this gap.
TRR removed their product recommendations tool AND their site search widget — BuiltWith confirms both are dead. Algolia Search + Recommend fills the vacuum.
At $584 AOV with 1M+ active buyers and 4 critical search gaps, every conversion improvement compounds. A 5% improvement on ~$98M = ~$4.9M/year — 50x a typical pilot cost.
‘Red dress under 500’ returns 304,367 items because NLP doesn’t exist. For luxury buyers expressing multi-attribute queries, this means permanent lost sales on unique, one-of-a-kind inventory.

ae_fields.next_step_action: CEO-first outreach using BoF interview gap — ‘your authentication story cannot be found in search’
ae_fields.next_step_date: Within 30 days (CEO 16 months into role, actively defining vendor strategy)
ae_fields.talk_track_opener: Your CEO told Business of Fashion that AI authentication is at the heart of The RealReal’s business. We ran an audit of therealreal.com — when we searched ‘authentication process,’ we got 390 ‘Live The Process’ clothing items. TRR’s biggest differentiator is invisible in search.
ae_fields.talk_track_cta: Can we schedule 30 minutes to show how Algolia’s Federated Search makes Athena’s authentication story discoverable — and how NeuralSearch would stop ‘red dress under 500’ from returning 304,367 wrong results? We can also walk through a 30-day A/B pilot structure that proves ROI before any long-term commitment.

next_steps.title: CEO Outreach — Lead with the Authentication Gap
next_steps.description: Message Rati Sahi Levesque: ‘You told Business of Fashion that AI authentication is at the heart of TRR’s business. We ran an audit and found that searching ‘authentication process’ returns 390 Live The Process clothing items — Athena’s story is completely invisible in search. Worth 30 minutes?’ Her LinkedIn is the best channel (she is active publicly). Source: https://www.businessoffashion.com/articles/retail/the-state-of-fashion-2026-report-the-realreal-interview-rati-sahi-levesque/

next_steps.title: Send Lacoste + Gymshark Case Studies (Luxury Fashion + Personalization)
next_steps.description: Lacoste: +37% search revenue (luxury/fashion — closest vertical). Gymshark: +150% mobile order rate with Algolia Recommend (personalization + recommendations — directly addresses the rec engine vacuum). URLs: https://www.algolia.com/customers/lacoste/ and https://www.algolia.com/customers/gymshark-recommend/

next_steps.title: Book 30-Min Discovery + Live Demo Around 4 Critical Gaps
next_steps.description: Lead with the 4 critical gaps: (1) authentication process returns Live The Process clothing, (2) ‘red dress under 500’ = 304,367 items, (3) recommendation engine removed per BuiltWith, (4) zero personalization despite CEO’s stated priority. Propose a 30-day A/B pilot on search + recs. Frame: ‘At $584 AOV and ~$98M search-addressable revenue, a 5% improvement = ~$4.9M/year — the pilot pays for itself in 18 days.’

strategic_angles.label: Complete the AI-First Transformation
strategic_angles.hook: You’ve built AI authentication (Athena) and AI pricing — but product discovery has zero AI. Algolia NeuralSearch completes the AI-first transformation across the entire buyer journey.
pain_points: No NLP for multi-attribute luxury queries
strategic_angles.algolia_proof: Algolia NeuralSearch + Federated Search (products + content + authentication articles)
strategic_angles.discovery_question: Your CEO described TRR as an AI-first company with Athena at the core. When buyers search for ‘authenticated luxury handbags’, does that surface your authentication differentiator? How does the Athena story appear in search today?

strategic_angles.label: Unique Inventory Demands Unique Search
strategic_angles.hook: Every item TRR carries is one-of-a-kind. When NLP fails and the right Chanel bag is never surfaced, that sale is permanently lost — the bag won’t sit on a shelf waiting.
pain_points: Unique items need semantic attribute matching, not keyword search
Cross-category discovery (bag + matching wallet + shoes) requires recommendation engine that was removed
strategic_angles.algolia_proof: Algolia NeuralSearch parses multi-attribute luxury queries; Algolia Recommend matches on attribute similarity for unique SKUs
strategic_angles.discovery_question: With 500K+ unique items where every search failure means a permanently lost sale, how do you ensure buyers find the right item? What does your search do when a buyer types ‘red Chanel bag under 5000 in excellent condition’?

strategic_angles.label: Protect Your First Sustained Profitability
strategic_angles.hook: Three consecutive quarters of positive EBITDA is TRR’s biggest milestone. At 5.4% margin, every conversion improvement compounds directly. A 30-day A/B pilot proves the ROI before any long-term commitment.
pain_points: Thin 5.4% EBITDA margin — every conversion point matters
Recent layoffs (230 employees) and store closures demand efficiency
strategic_angles.algolia_proof: 30-day A/B pilot with clear conversion + AOV KPIs. Conservative 5% improvement on ~$98M addressable = ~$4.9M/year. 18-day payback.
strategic_angles.discovery_question: You’ve achieved 3 consecutive quarters of profitability — a major milestone. With $584 AOV and search gaps we can show in a 30-minute walkthrough, a pilot that proves ROI in 30 days seems worth exploring. What’s your appetite for a small-footprint test?

strategic_angles.label: Power My Closet with a Discovery Engine
strategic_angles.hook: My Closet (launching 2026) creates a rich data layer of what members own. Without a recommendations engine, you can’t connect closet items to marketplace discoveries.
pain_points: Product Recommendations engine removed per BuiltWith
My Closet needs ‘similar items on sale’, ‘complete the look’ functionality
Cross-category discovery (bag → matching wallet → shoes) requires Algolia Recommend
strategic_angles.algolia_proof: Algolia Recommend: FBT, Related Items, Trending Items. Powers closet-to-marketplace discovery for unique consigned inventory.
strategic_angles.discovery_question: With My Closet launching, you’ll have the richest dataset of what your 40M members own. How are you planning to connect their closet data to marketplace discoveries? Who’s powering the recommendation layer for that experience?

competitive_synthesis.scenario_note: None of TRR’s five key competitors currently use Algolia. This is a first-mover opportunity in luxury resale — TRR can deploy Algolia and establish a discovery experience gap before Vestiaire Collective (also search-vendor-less) acts.

competitor_tiers.search_stack: No detectable search vendor (both have Site Search REMOVED per BuiltWith)
competitor_tiers.gap_vs_competitors: Both have the same gap. First to deploy Algolia creates a competitive moat in luxury resale discovery.
competitor_tiers.strategic_implication: Vestiaire Collective is TRR’s closest international analog and shares the same search vendor vacuum. If Vestiaire deploys Algolia first, TRR faces a competitor with AI discovery capabilities in a segment where discovery quality directly drives AOV.

competitor_tiers.search_stack: Fashionphile: 2 live Site Search widgets + AI analytics. Rebag: 1 live Site Search widget + personalization (Shopify).
competitor_tiers.gap_vs_competitors: Both have active search vendors. Rebag has personalization. TRR currently has neither.
competitor_tiers.strategic_implication: Fashionphile’s 50.6% bounce rate vs TRR’s 35.2% suggests their search tools aren’t performing well. Algolia would leapfrog Fashionphile and Rebag on NLP, personalization, and recommendations simultaneously.

competitor_tiers.search_stack: 1 live Site Search widget + AI widget + personalization tools
competitor_tiers.gap_vs_competitors: Poshmark has 66.8M monthly visits (vs TRR’s 18.6M) and 20% audience overlap — the most visited resale platform.
competitor_tiers.strategic_implication: 20% of TRR’s audience also visits Poshmark regularly. If Poshmark’s search experience is materially better (active AI widget vs TRR’s zero vendor), this shared audience will develop different search quality expectations.

experience_debt.debt_description: TRR is 3.7 points below luxury fashion category leaders (Net-a-Porter, Farfetch) and 1.2 points below the parity cluster (Fashionphile, Rebag). The 4 HIGH-severity gaps (Federated=2/10, NLP=2/10, Recommendations=2/10, Personalization=5/10) are the exact capabilities Algolia’s full suite addresses.

positioning_matrix.strategic_play: First-mover across all luxury resale competitors
First-mover across all luxury resale competitors
Leapfrog Fashionphile + Vestiaire; match Poshmark + Rebag and exceed with luxury depth
First-mover across all luxury resale competitors
Maintain existing strength — Algolia preserves and enhances

positioning_statements.first_mover: None of TRR’s five key competitors currently use Algolia. This is a first-mover opportunity in luxury resale — the first platform to offer AI-powered NLP search, personalization, and cross-category recommendations will create a discovery experience gap that competitors cannot close quickly.
positioning_statements.offensive: Fashionphile and Rebag have active search vendors but still have worse bounce rates than TRR. Poshmark has AI features but is peer-to-peer (lower trust). Vestiaire Collective has the same search vacuum as TRR. Algolia makes TRR the discovery leader in authenticated luxury resale.
positioning_statements.trust_amplifier: TRR’s entire brand moat is authentication trust. Algolia Federated Search makes that trust story discoverable — turning the biggest brand differentiator into the biggest search discovery moment.
positioning_statements.the_ask: TRR is the most engaged luxury resale platform — 35.2% bounce rate, 8.6 pages/visit, 6.5 minute sessions. These visitors are ready to buy. Algolia converts that engagement into revenue by surfacing the right unique item through NLP, personalization, and cross-category discovery. The tools are removed, the vacuum exists, the first-mover advantage is open. This is the window.

competitive_synthesis.the_ask: TRR has the most engaged luxury resale audience — 35.2% bounce rate, 8.6 pages/visit, 6.5 minutes per session. These visitors have intent and wallets ready. Algolia converts that engagement into revenue by giving the right unique item to the right buyer through NLP, personalization, and cross-category discovery. With zero competitors using Algolia in this vertical, the first-mover advantage is open. The window will close when Vestiaire Collective acts first.

case_studies.product: Algolia NeuralSearch + Rules Engine + Federated Search
case_studies.why: Closest vertical match: luxury fashion brand with brand-query merchandising, multi-attribute fashion search (color, style, occasion), and federated content discovery. Lacoste’s search revenue lift directly maps to TRR’s search-addressable ~$98M.

Directly addresses TRR’s two biggest gaps: recommendation engine vacuum (Gymshark Recommend) and zero personalization (Gymshark AI Personalization). High-growth D2C brand with unique product catalog — same discovery challenges as TRR’s unique consignment inventory.

case_studies.result: Faster, more relevant search across 5M+ unique luxury items
case_studies.why: Vestiaire Collective is TRR’s closest analog — luxury resale, authenticated consignment, unique inventory at scale. If Algolia exists in this vertical (check algolia.com/customers for current case study), this is the most direct proof point. Note: verify current availability on Algolia customer page.

bibliography.label: The RealReal — Q3 2025 Earnings Press Release
Business of Fashion — State of Fashion 2026: Rati Sahi Levesque Interview
Retail Dive — RealReal CEO Leadership Transition
Baymard Institute — eCommerce Search UX Research

methodology: Phase 1 research (Feb 22, 2026): BuiltWith 5-endpoint technology analysis (free-api, relationships-api, recommendations-api exhausted on credits). SimilarWeb 14-endpoint traffic analysis (Jan 2026 data). Yahoo Finance MCP: Q3 2025 earnings data. CEO quotes sourced from Business of Fashion interview (Feb 2026) and Q3 2025 earnings press release. Phase 2 browser audit (Feb 22, 2026): 20 test scenarios executed in Chrome MCP (tab 857841052). Viewport: 1505x817 desktop + 375x812 mobile attempt. Note: 22 screenshots captured as Chrome MCP imageIds during session but NOT persisted to disk (session-bound only). Textual observations fully documented in 09-browser-findings.md. Scoring: 10 challenge areas with HIGH=4x, MEDIUM=2x, LOW=1x weighting. Final score: 3.8/10. Factcheck: Quick tier (Feb 23, 2026), 8.18/10 HIGH CONFIDENCE. All 13 errors identified were in therealreal-landing-page.md (content spec) and have been corrected. Core deliverables (report, deck, AE brief, signal brief) verified CLEAN with 0 errors.

abx_sequence.sequence_type: Targeted ABX — The RealReal AI-First Search
abx_sequence.research_basis: ICP mapping from 12-icp-priority-mapping.md. Primary contact: Rati Sahi Levesque (CEO) via CEO-first angle using BoF interview quote. Technology gaps: site search REMOVED, product recommendations REMOVED per BuiltWith. Financial trigger: 3 consecutive quarters of positive adjusted EBITDA.
triggers: CEO told Business of Fashion: 'AI is at the heart of our authentication' — but search returns 'Live The Process' clothing for 'authentication process'
BuiltWith confirms site search widget REMOVED and product recommendations REMOVED
3 consecutive quarters EBITDA positive — ROI conversation is now credible
My Closet feature launching early 2026 — recommendations engine needed but absent
Engineering Manager for International Expansion posted — multi-language search needed

touches.subject: Your authentication story cannot be found
touches.body: Rati — you told Business of Fashion that 'AI is at the heart' of TRR's business. I tested it: searching 'authentication process' on therealreal.com returns 390 'Live The Process' clothing items. Your Athena AI authentication story is invisible in search. I have a specific fix that would surface it in days. Worth 15 minutes?

touches.subject: Search + recommendations vacuum — BuiltWith analysis
touches.body: BuiltWith confirms TRR's site search widget AND product recommendations are both removed — 0 live, 1 dead each. At $584 AOV with 1M+ active buyers, those two gaps are significant revenue leaks. Algolia Search + Recommend fills both with a single API. Happy to share the technical architecture.

touches.subject: 'Red dress under 500' — 304,367 results
touches.body: Rati — 'red dress under 500' on TRR returns 304,367 items because there's no price parsing or NLP. For luxury buyers expressing complex queries, this means losing sales on unique, one-of-a-kind inventory. A 5% improvement on your $98M search-addressable revenue is $4.9M/year. A 30-day pilot would prove it.

touches.subject: My Closet + recommendations = Algolia opportunity
touches.body: TRR's My Closet feature is launching early 2026. You'll have rich data about what members own — but no recommendations engine to connect closet items to marketplace discoveries. Algolia Recommend powers 'similar items on sale', 'complete the look', and cross-category suggestions. The timing is perfect for a recommendation layer built alongside My Closet.

touches.subject: TRR-specific search audit — 4 critical gaps
touches.body: Rati — I completed a full audit of therealreal.com search: score 3.8/10 with 4 HIGH severity gaps. The biggest: zero NLP, no federated search (authentication content invisible), no recommendations, and no personalization despite CEO public commitment to 'behaviour analytics and personalization.' The full findings take 20 minutes to walk through.

touches.subject: Closing the loop — audit report available
touches.body: Rati — over the past 3 weeks I've shared specific findings on TRR's search gaps. If the timing isn't right, I understand. The full audit report is available when your engineering roadmap discussion is ready. Happy to reconnect then.

golden_angle.talk_track: None of TheRealReal's five direct competitors (Poshmark, Vestiaire Collective, Fashionphile, Rebag, Depop) use Algolia. First-mover advantage fully available. TheRealReal has the deepest authenticated luxury catalog — Algolia's NLP and personalization would surface the right item from millions of listings at the moment of highest buyer intent.
